# Player top-down placeholder sprite sheets

**Functional blockout stand-in — not final art.** Generated by
`tools/gen_player_placeholder_sheet.py` so the player can be wired into an
`AnimatedSprite2D` now, replacing the single `Polygon2D`. Swap in real painterly
art later (see `CHARACTER_ASSET_PROMPTS_PASS_11.md`); the slicing below stays the
same as long as the grid matches.

To regenerate: `python tools/gen_player_placeholder_sheet.py`

## Grid

- **Cell:** 96 × 96 px, transparent background, no baked shadow.
- **Rows (top → bottom):** `down`, `up`, `left`, `right`  ← facing direction
  (canonical order, matches `CHARACTER_SPRITE_SPEC.md`).
- **Columns:** animation frames (left → right).
- Figure centred; feet near a common baseline (~y 66 within the cell); origin =
  cell centre (aligns to the player body centre).

| File | Size | H frames | V frames | Frames/dir |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `player_idle.png`   | 384 × 384 | 4 | 4 | 4 |
| `player_walk.png`   | 576 × 384 | 6 | 4 | 6 |
| `player_attack.png` | 384 × 384 | 4 | 4 | 4 |
| `player_hurt.png`   | 192 × 384 | 2 | 4 | 2 |

`player_topdown_preview.png` is a stacked eyeball reference only — do **not** use
it in-game.

## Slicing into SpriteFrames (Godot 4)

For each file: *SpriteFrames → Add frames from sprite sheet*, set **Horizontal =
H frames**, **Vertical = 4**. Each row is one direction. Create animations named
`<anim>_<dir>`, e.g. `idle_down`, `walk_left`, `attack_up`, `hurt_right`. Loop
`idle_*` and `walk_*`; leave `attack_*` / `hurt_*` non-looping.

Suggested `AnimatedSprite2D` node name: **`Visual`** (so the existing hurt-flash
`modulate` keeps working — see `CHARACTER_ART_INTEGRATION_CHECKLIST.md`). Start
`scale` near **0.55** and nudge so the body matches the 18 × 22 collision box.

## Direction order

Row order is the canonical **down / up / left / right**, matching
`CHARACTER_SPRITE_SPEC.md` and the Hollow placeholder sheets. Keep the
`SpriteFrames` animation names (`walk_down`, etc.) as the runtime source of truth
so row order never matters in code.
